items.mistake: Logging training accuracy as the primary metric for run comparison
items.fix: Log `val_*` or `test_*` metrics with the same key on every run; document which split each key uses in params.
items.why: Train scores favor overfit configs — comparison should rank generalization, not memorization.
items.example: Wrong: compare runs on `train_f1=0.99`. Right: `mlflow.log_metric("val_f1", f1_val)` after evaluating on the held-out split.

items.mistake: Using different metric or param key names across runs in the same sweep
items.fix: Define a shared logging module with constants (`METRIC_VAL_F1 = "val_f1"`) used by every script variant.
items.why: MLflow compare and W&B sort/filter rely on identical keys — typos create silent empty columns.
items.example: Run A logs `val_auc`; Run B logs `validation_auc` — descending sort by `val_auc` drops Run B entirely.

items.mistake: Nested or multiple active MLflow runs without context managers
items.fix: Use `with mlflow.start_run():` or explicitly `mlflow.end_run()`; one training job → one top-level run.
items.why: Orphan or nested runs attach metrics to the wrong ID, corrupting comparison tables.
items.example: Calling `start_run()` twice without ending leaves metrics on an inner run while you print the outer `run_id`.

items.mistake: Forgetting `wandb.finish()` or leaving W&B runs in crashed state
items.fix: Use try/finally with `run.finish()`, or `wandb.init` as context manager where supported.
items.why: Unclosed runs show as 'running' in the UI, break sweep agents, and may lose buffered metrics.
items.example: Notebook kernel dies after `wandb.init` — run stays green forever until manually stopped in UI.
